hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 1 (General Provisions) — the title that explains how the whole Code works: how it's adopted and amended, how to read its three-part section numbers, the standard definitions, and the general penalty that applies when a chapter doesn't state its own. This is the Code's instruction manual. Sections marked expanding are being sourced verbatim; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: 1 · How the Code works
sections.note: The mechanics every other title relies on.
sections.html: Title 1 (General Provisions) adopts the Maui County Code and sets the rules for reading it. Section numbers use a three-part decimal system — Title.Chapter.Section (e.g. 2.12.040 is section .040 in chapter 2.12 of Title 2). Title 1 also carries the Code's standard definitions and the general penalty — the default penalty that applies to a violation when its own chapter doesn't prescribe one. When you read any ordinance, Title 1 is the lens that tells you how it's structured and enforced.

source.html: Maui County Code Title 1 (General Provisions).

sections.title: 2 · What Title 1 contains
sections.note: The structural pieces — verbatim section numbers being sourced.

rows.v: Adopts the Code; rules for how it's read and amended
Standard meanings used across all titles
Default penalty when a chapter states none

sections.title: 3 · The lens — clarity as access
sections.note: Labeled analysis — the Code's own rules read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the Council enacts and codifies ordinances and the Code governs the County; Title 1 makes the body of law coherent and uniformly enforceable. The Charter frames general provisions as the connective tissue of self-government.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, law that the people cannot read is law that excludes them. The lens reads Title 1's job — definitions, numbering, a general penalty — as a duty of legibility: the Code should be understandable to the resident it binds, not only to lawyers.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: A code only governs justly if the governed can read it. This entire plain-language service exists to honor that — Title 1's clarity rules, made actually clear. Same title, read two ways.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this whole page is Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a plain-language Code-reading assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
